purpose: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion PDE running per-vertex on a polygonal mesh; produces self-organizing Turing-pattern blobs/spots/labyrinths plus optional displacement-along-normal driven by chemical concentration.
visual_result: Plane: black surface gradually gains white blobby vertex-map patterns. Sphere: starts plain, grows yellow horn-bumps where chemical crosses displacement threshold. Patterns continuously evolve frame-by-frame.
input_geometry: Sphere primitive (Advanced) and Plane primitive (Simple), both fed via legacyobjectaccess.

hosts.role: Advanced setup — full RD with exposure curve + spline-driven displacement
Simple setup — pedagogical core: just chemical diffusion → vertex map

annotation_storage_pattern: scaffold@ nodes inside the graph carry effectivename labels acting as section headers; no Annotation tags on OM objects in this scene.

am_sliders_advanced.name: Add new Chemical
am_sliders_advanced.controls: chemical injection rate from Random Field

key_connections.purpose: PER-FRAME FEEDBACK LOOP — Memory's Chemical Array output feeds back into its own input port via _0 self-binding. This is how Neutron carries state across frames without explicit time integration.
Per-vertex query position drives the K-nearest-neighbor lookup.
The mesh whose points are searched for neighbors.
AM-exposed K parameter for the K-NN Laplacian.
Final output sink — vertex map writes back onto host geometry.
Host geometry emission — analogous to root.geometryout in nodespace.scene.
OM-to-graph bridge — feeds the input Sphere/Plane geometry into the Neutron graph.
Field paints vertex selection data on input mesh; getvertexselectiondata reads it as scalar field for chemical injection.

output_routing.via: Set Vertex Map → Geometry Op → host geometry input
output_routing.host_visibility_path: 180420500 capsule renders root output as classic mesh in OM
output_routing.notes: In Neutron, the geometry@ node is the canonical output sink (analogue of set_property→root.geometryout in the older nodespace).

interesting_types: Memory primitive — per-frame state buffer with self-feedback wire (out._0 → in._0)
K-nearest-neighbor index array + per-neighbor distance array (output of nearestneighbor)
Spline curve port (Displacement Profil) — AM-surfaces as a curve editor
Vertex selection data exposed via getvertexselectiondata — bridges C4D Fields into the graph

core_idea_nodes.name: Difussion Solver
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Per-frame state-retention primitive. Its Chemical Array output feeds back into its own input — that's THE mechanism that makes the PDE persistent across frames. The single most important node in the scene.

core_idea_nodes.name: Closest Points
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Provides the discrete Laplacian stencil via K-nearest-neighbor lookup. Robust to mesh quality (works on point clouds too). Tunable via Neighbors Min/Max/Count + Maximum Distance.

core_idea_nodes.name: Iterate Collection
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Per-vertex iteration that drives Closest Points. Without this, the K-NN would only fire once.

core_idea_nodes.name: Blur React value
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Per-iteration-within-frame state for the blur kernel (different from Memory's per-frame state). Demonstrates that Neutron has TWO levels of state-feedback: spatial (LCV) and temporal (Memory).

core_idea_nodes.name: Bevel Profil
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Spline-typed AM port — lets the artist author an arbitrary displacement profile curve in the Attribute Manager. Demonstrates the Spline-typed-param surface that cinema4d-mcp does not yet synthesize.

scaffolding_nodes: scaffold (×7 — section labels: Get Chemical / Get Neighbors / Get Exposure / Calculate Displacement Amount / Assemble Arrays / Post Blur Chemical / Displace along Normal Direction)
multransform_5 / combine / mat / sqrpart / sqrtrans / vectrans (root template internals)
what_is_clever: Memory primitive's self-feedback wire IS the per-frame state mechanism — no explicit time-integration loop needed. This is THE keystone insight.
K-NN-based Laplacian instead of mesh-edge Laplacian — works on any point cloud, robust to mesh quality.
Random Field paints vertex selection on input mesh; getvertexselectiondata reads it as initial-condition seed inside the graph. Clean Field→Graph bridge.
Spline-curve-driven displacement profile — artist-authored exposure mapping from PDE concentration to visible displacement.
Two-level state hierarchy: Memory (per-frame) + LCV (per-iteration-within-frame). Different feedback timescales for different purposes.
Conditional gates (if@ nodes 'If Post Blur', 'If Displacement') let users toggle expensive sections off.

gotchas.issue: Plugin ID 180420500 hosts use net.maxon.neutron.nodespace, NOT net.maxon.nodespace.scene. Different graph engine entirely.
gotchas.workaround: Use NEUTRON = 'net.maxon.neutron.nodespace' as the nodespace ID for type 180420500. Add this to gotcha #56.

gotchas.issue: Annotations not stored as Annotation tags on OM objects; instead encoded as effectivename on scaffold@ nodes inside the graph.
gotchas.evidence: walk_ann() returned 0 annotations across the OM tree; scaffold nodes had effectivename like 'Get Chemical' / 'Get Neighbors' acting as section labels.
gotchas.workaround: When mining annotations from a Neutron-engine scene, query scaffold nodes' effectivename property in addition to walking OM tags.

gotchas.issue: AM-exposed slider names come back as '(unnamed)' from host.GetDescription() because Neutron's descid encoding uses long byte-encoded segment chains; DESC_NAME isn't surfaced at the leaf level.
gotchas.evidence: Probed all 72 (777, …) descid leaves on the Advanced host; every name field was '(unnamed)'.
gotchas.workaround: Read slider names via the floatingio nodes' effectivename property instead of via host.GetDescription. The graph-side names are the authoritative source.

gotchas.issue: legacyobjectaccess node may have stale name (e.g., named 'Sphere' on the Simple host where input is actually 'Plane Simple').
gotchas.evidence: Simple host's legacyobjectaccess.effectivename = 'Sphere' but its Object input wires to root.in receiving 'Plane Simple' from OM.
gotchas.workaround: Don't trust legacyobjectaccess node's name; trust the Object port's wired-from input.

rebuild_recipe: 1. Create a Scene Nodes Generator (180420500) host. Connect an input mesh (Sphere/Plane/whatever).
2. Inside the Neutron graph, add legacyobjectaccess wired to root.input — bridges OM input mesh into the graph.
3. Add containeriteration consuming the input mesh's points array — the per-vertex iterator.
4. Add nearestneighbor with: Geometry ← input mesh, Query Position ← containeriteration.out, Maximum Neighbors ← AM-exposed Int slider.
5. Add memory (the keystone). Wire Chemical Array.out._0 → Chemical Array.in._0 — this is the self-feedback that makes state persist across frames.

6. For each per-vertex iteration: read previous chemical at neighbor indices (readvalueatindex), compute Laplacian (sum/avg), apply Gray-Scott reaction step (arithmetic chain), write new chemical via set into a per-vertex array.

7. Optional injection: add a doc-level Random Field. Its vertex selection on the input mesh is read inside the graph via getvertexselectiondata — provides the noisy initial condition that breaks the PDE's symmetry.
8. Output: pass the final per-vertex chemical array through Set Vertex Map → geometry@ → root.geometry input.
9. Synthesize 4-18 typed AM ports on root: Float for diffusion/reaction rates, Int for neighbor counts, Bool for toggles, Spline for displacement-profile curve.

10. (Advanced) For displacement output: add another containeriteration over points, sample chemical via readvalueatindex, multiply by Displacement Profil spline(chemical) × Displacement Height, displace along the vertex Normal — write back to vertex positions before the final emit.

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: memory_feedback_pde_solver
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Run any per-vertex PDE on a polygonal mesh with K-nearest-neighbor diffusion stencil; state persists across frames via memory's self-feedback wire.
nodes: geometrypoints (extract point array from input geometry)
containeriteration (per-vertex iterator over the point array)
nearestneighbor (K-NN per vertex — provides the discrete Laplacian stencil)
memory (per-vertex state buffer, self-fed via .out._0 → .in._0 — THE keystone)
arithmetic (the reaction step: u' = u + dt*(D*Laplacian + f(u,v)))
set (write per-vertex result to a vertex map / point attribute)
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.value_proposition: Generalizes far beyond Gray-Scott: same 9-node skeleton runs heat equation, Cahn-Hilliard, custom paint-spread, voxel-style propagation, growth simulations, cellular automata. Memory's self-feedback wire is the ONE primitive that lets a graph carry per-frame state in Neutron. Massive capability gain for tiny graph.
blockers_to_recipe_implementation: cinema4d-mcp scene_nodes_synthesize_port doesn't yet support net.maxon.neutron.nodespace — currently scoped to net.maxon.nodespace.scene only.
Memory primitive's self-feedback wiring pattern needs a dedicated helper since it's bidirectional on the same port.
C4D Field-to-graph bridge via getvertexselectiondata + vertex selection painting needs documenting.

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: Type 180420500 (Scene Nodes Generator) ≠ 180420600 (Nodes Mesh simple). Different graph engines (Neutron vs scene). dissect_capsule and add_node need to detect both.
memory@ is THE feedback-loop primitive. Worth a dedicated recipe and a synthesizer helper.
nearestneighbor@ + containeriteration@ is the canonical per-vertex spatial-query pattern.
C4D Field objects bridge into Neutron via vertex selection on the input mesh, read via getvertexselectiondata.
Spline-typed AM sliders exist; cinema4d-mcp synthesize_port doesn't yet support them.
scaffold@ nodes carry section-label annotations; mine effectivename when extracting documentation.
